UCL 2nd legs · PSG vs LIV · Bayern vs RMA

golf.result: Masters R3: McIlroy & Young tied -11 · Burns -10 · Lowry -9 (hole-in-one R3) · Final round TODAY
golf.note: McIlroy/Young final pairing 2:25 ET · CBS from Augusta · 11 players within 5 shots

nascar.result: Food City 500 at Bristol TODAY (3 ET · FS1) — Blaney on pole · Reddick leads Cup standings
nascar.note: Zilisch won Saturday's Xfinity race at Bristol · Short track chaos expected

topStories.headline: McIlroy's six-shot lead is gone — he and Cameron Young are tied heading into the final round
body: The most dramatic moving day in Masters memory. Rory McIlroy arrived at Augusta National on Saturday with a six-shot lead — the largest in tournament history after 36 holes — and watched it evaporate. He doubled the par-4 11th and bogeyed the 12th, shooting a 1-over 73 while Cameron Young went low with an 8-birdie 65. They head into Sunday's final round tied at 11-under.

Sam Burns (–10) and Shane Lowry (–9, including a hole-in-one at No. 6) are right in it. Scottie Scheffler shot 65 and is at –7, five back. Eleven players are within five shots. McIlroy will either become the first back-to-back Masters champion since Tiger Woods in 2002, or he'll have squandered the biggest 36-hole lead in the tournament's history. Final pairing tees off at 2:25 ET on CBS.

topStories.headline: 54 points with Luka and Reaves out — LeBron James is somehow still doing this
body: LeBron James, 41 years old and playing the second night of a back-to-back, scored 54 points on 63.6% shooting as the Los Angeles Lakers crushed Phoenix 101–73 on Saturday. Luka Dončić and Austin Reaves were both out with injuries — LeBron simply refused to lose. The win gave the Lakers home-court advantage in the first round of the playoffs and marked the 15th win in their last 19 games. Along the way, LeBron became the fourth player in NBA history to record 12,000 career assists.

topStories.headline: Food City 500 today — Reddick, Hamlin, and Elliott headline a packed Bristol field
body: Tyler Reddick leads the Cup Series standings with four wins, but Bristol Motor Speedway plays by its own rules. Ryan Blaney won the pole and will lead the field to green. Connor Zilisch dominated yesterday's Xfinity race on the same track, so fast laps are possible. The Food City 500 starts at 3 ET on FS1 — one of the most unpredictable events on the NASCAR calendar.

stories.headline: LeBron carrying the Lakers — and it's not close
body: With Luka Dončić and Austin Reaves sidelined, the Lakers needed their elder statesman to step up — and LeBron delivered one of the most jaw-dropping performances of the season. 54 points on 21-of-33 shooting in a 101–73 win over Phoenix. Luke Kennard added 19 points in support. In Sacramento, Brandon Podziemski posted a career-high 30 as the Kings beat Golden State 124–118.

stories.headline: Regular season finale tonight — the full bracket sets by Sunday evening
body: The NBA's regular season wraps up Sunday evening with a full slate. Detroit (Pistons) hold the East's top seed, Boston the 2-seed, New York the 3-seed. The Western Conference picture is largely set as well — OKC is the 1-seed, with Minnesota, Denver, and the Lakers expected to fill the top four. The first round of the NBA Playoffs begins April 18. Seedings could still shift tonight.

stories.headline: Kings win fourth straight — the West wild card race is razor thin
body: The Los Angeles Kings blanked the Edmonton Oilers on Saturday and have won four consecutive games to put themselves in strong position for the West's second wild card spot. Six days remain in the regular season, and the standings from 7th through 10th in the West are separated by just a handful of points. The regular season ends April 16, with the playoffs beginning April 19.

stories.headline: Hurricanes rolling, Sabres lead Atlantic — the East bracket is taking shape
body: Carolina beat Utah to extend their lead in the Eastern Conference. The Buffalo Sabres are holding the Atlantic Division lead as the season winds down — a remarkable run for a franchise that missed the playoffs for years. Cole Caufield's 50th goal powered Montreal to the postseason, and the Canadiens enter as one of the East's more dangerous wild-card entries.

stories.headline: Dodgers and Yankees flying — Ohtani's on-base streak at 40
body: Two weeks into the MLB season, the Dodgers (9–3) and Yankees (8–2) are the class of their respective leagues. New York has allowed just 8 runs in their first 9 games — historically stingy. Shohei Ohtani has reached base in 40 consecutive games for the Dodgers, an MLB-best streak extending back to spring training. The Brewers (8–3) remain the NL's other early-season standout.

stories.headline: Liverpool need three goals at the Parc des Princes — PSG are the heavy favorites
body: Liverpool face a 2–0 first-leg deficit when they travel to Paris on April 14. It's not mathematically impossible — but PSG's defensive discipline and depth makes it extremely unlikely. Désiré Doué and Khvicha Kvaratskhelia are clinical in big moments, and PSG haven't conceded more than one goal in a UCL match all season. The second tie — Atlético vs Barcelona on the same night — sees Barça needing to overturn a 2–0 deficit after being reduced to 10 men.

stories.headline: Real Madrid's Allianz test — can Vinícius lead the comeback?
body: Real Madrid must win at the Allianz Arena on April 15 to advance. Bayern Munich have conceded just 3 goals at home all season. But Madrid have done the impossible before — this is a club built for late drama. Vinícius Júnior scored both goals in the first leg and will be the focus of Bayern's defensive setup. Arsenal vs Sporting CP on the same night sees Arsenal comfortably placed with a 1–0 lead to defend.

stories.headline: Antonelli 72, Russell 63 — the title fight is already a Mercedes civil war
body: Kimi Antonelli leads the 2026 F1 championship by 9 points over teammate George Russell after three races. Russell won Australia; Antonelli took China and Japan. Charles Leclerc sits third for Ferrari at 49 points. Mercedes have dominated the season opener with their 2026 regulation machinery, but the intra-team battle between a 19-year-old phenom and an experienced race winner makes for compelling viewing. The Miami Grand Prix is the next stop, May 1–3.

stories.headline: Clay season opens Monday — Sinner arrives at Monte Carlo as the clear favorite
body: The Monte Carlo Masters begins Monday on the red clay of the Principality. Jannik Sinner arrives fresh from his Sunshine Double — two Masters wins without dropping a set — and is the overwhelming favorite. Carlos Alcaraz, three-time Roland Garros champion, is the main challenger. This is his best surface. The Monte Carlo draw will be released Sunday, setting up the first major clay-season clashes ahead of the French Open on May 25.

stories.headline: The greatest final-round setup in Masters history — 11 players within five shots
body: Augusta National has a final round for the ages. Rory McIlroy and Cameron Young co-lead at –11 and will play together in the last group. Sam Burns (–10) and Shane Lowry (–9) are right there — Lowry's hole-in-one at the par-3 sixth on Saturday was the shot of the tournament. Scottie Scheffler, world No. 1, is at –7 and has shot 65 twice this week.

McIlroy is chasing history — the fourth back-to-back Masters champion ever — but now carries the additional pressure of having surrendered a record lead. Young, 28, is hunting his first major. The last time this many players were this close to the lead entering Sunday was 1954. Coverage begins at 2 PM ET on CBS.

stories.headline: Food City 500 preview — Reddick is dominant but Bristol humbles everyone
body: Ryan Blaney claimed the pole for today's Food City 500 at Bristol Motor Speedway (3 ET, FS1). Tyler Reddick leads the championship with four wins but has never won at Bristol. Denny Hamlin and Chase Elliott are the drivers to watch — both excel on concrete short tracks. Yesterday, Connor Zilisch dominated the Xfinity Series race on the same surface. Bristol rewards aggression, punishes patience, and produces wrecks in bunches. Don't miss it.
